role: Minister of Foreign Affairs (2024–present); lead nuclear negotiator
bio: Abbas Araghchi is Iran's current Foreign Minister under [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian). A career diplomat, he served as Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs and was Iran's lead technical nuclear negotiator during the JCPOA talks under [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif). He was one of the architects of the 2015 nuclear deal. After the deal's collapse he remained at the [Ministry of Foreign Affairs](/orgs/ministry-of-foreign-affairs) as a senior official. His appointment as FM under Pezeshkian was seen as a signal of continuity with the reformist-era foreign policy school. His early tenure has been shaped by the post-2026 succession crisis and Iran's recalibrated position under [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei).
editorial_notes: Born 1966 in Isfahan. Lead nuclear negotiator 2013–2021. Appointed FM August 2024 by Pezeshkian.
